name: Parker House Rolls
recipeIngredient: 2 packages Active Dry Yeast (or 2 tablespoons Bulk Active Dry Yeast)
recipeInstructions:
1. Pour the scalded milk over the sugar, butter, and salt in a mixing bowl.
2. Cool to lukewarm.
3. Sprinkle the yeast over the lukewarm water and stir until dissolved.
4. Add 2 cups of flour and the yeast mixture to the milk mixture.
5. Beat with an electric mixer on medium speed until smooth (about 2 minutes).
6. Gradually add enough of the remaining flour to form a soft dough.
7. Cover and let rest for 15 minutes.
8. Knead the dough a few times on a lightly floured surface until it is no longer sticky.
9. Roll out to a 1/2-inch thickness.
10. Cut into rounds using a floured biscuit cutter.
11. Brush the rounds with the remaining melted butter and crease each roll in the center with the dull edge of a knife.
12. Fold the rolls over on the crease and press the edges together lightly.
13. Place on greased baking sheets and cover.
14. Let rise in a warm place until almost doubled (about 30 minutes).
15. Bake in a preheated 400°F oven for 10 minutes or until golden brown.
16. Remove from the baking sheets and cool on wire racks.
